title: spec-command dictionary
description: Collection of symbols such as variables, constants, and functions, used by the VS Code spec-command extension for code completion and navigation

identifier.description: Identifier of the dictionary. This is used for internal management and should be unique across all dictionaries.

scope.description: Scope of the dictionary. This also indicates where the dictionary is stored.

name.description: Name of the dictionary. It should not include any line breaks.

description.description: Description of the dictionary. Markdown syntax is supported. It can contain multiple lines and paragraphs. Markdown syntax for both inline and block decorations is supported.

signature.description: Signature. In case of variables and constants, this is usually the name of the variable or constant. In case of functions, this is usually the function signature including parameters and surrounding parentheses.

available.description: Specifies the version range in which the item is available.

deprecated.description: Specifies the version range in which the item is deprecated.

snippet.description: Code snippet to be inserted when the item is selected in code completion.

overloads.description: Overloads of the item, only used for functions.

signature.description: Signature of the overload. This is usually the function signature including parameters and surrounding parentheses.
